stages.stageTitle: STAGE 1: The Foundation / The Engine & Environment

weeks.weekTitle: WEEK 1: Command Line, Git, and Web Primitives
weeks.focus: Terminal commands, Git version control, HTML5, and CSS3 (Flexbox/Grid).
concepts: Git & GitHub (Init, Add, Commit, Push, Branching)

microProjects.title: 1. CLI & Git Sandbox
microProjects.whatToBuild: A local text-based file system tree tracked via Git.
microProjects.conceptsUsed: mkdir, cd, git init, git add, git commit, git checkout -b.
microProjects.action: After learning CLI/Git -> Build a local repository with 5 linked text files, create a new branch, modify a file, and merge it. Push to GitHub. Write a README detailing what commands failed initially.

microProjects.title: 2. Linktree Clone
microProjects.whatToBuild: A responsive profile page with multiple external links.
microProjects.action: After learning Flexbox -> Build a Linktree clone. Host it on GitHub Pages.

microProjects.title: 3. Static Analytics Dashboard
microProjects.whatToBuild: A dashboard layout with a sidebar, header, and a 3x3 grid of "metric" cards.
microProjects.action: After learning CSS Grid -> Build the Dashboard layout. No JavaScript yet, just pure structure.

microProjects.title: 4. Semantic Profile
microProjects.whatToBuild: Create a 1-page HTML document about yourself. Use Flexbox to center a main card. No external CSS libraries.

advancedProject.title: Personal Portfolio V1 (Static)
advancedProject.features: About section, Projects grid (placeholders), Contact form UI.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Semantic markup, CSS Grid for layout, Flexbox for alignment, CSS Variables for theming (Light/Dark mode via CSS classes).

openSource.whatToChange: Add yourself to a contributor list to learn the strict PR workflow.
openSource.contributionFlow: 1. Fork repo. 2. Create branch (git checkout -b add-my-name). 3. Make change. 4. Commit with clear message. 5. Create Pull Request.

resources.read: Chapters 1 (Getting Started) and 2 (Git Basics).
resources.skip: Chapters on Git Internals and Server setup.

resources.read: Flexbox and Grids sections.
resources.skip: Floats, Positioning (skim only), Legacy layout methods.

resources.read: Getting Started and HTML text fundamentals.
Read entirely. Flexbox is the only layout tool you need right now.
Chapter 1 (Getting Started) and Chapter 2 (Git Basics).

weeks.weekTitle: WEEK 2: JavaScript Core, DOM, and Node.js Intro
weeks.focus: Variables, Datatypes, Functions, Arrays, Objects, connecting JS to HTML via the DOM, and transitioning to Node.js.

microProjects.title: 1. CLI String Manipulator
microProjects.whatToBuild: A simple Node script that takes a string and returns it reversed, capitalized, and counted.
microProjects.action: After learning JS Functions -> Build String Manipulator. Run it in terminal using node app.js.

microProjects.title: 2. Interactive To-Do List
microProjects.whatToBuild: A web app to add, delete, and mark tasks as complete.
microProjects.action: After learning the DOM -> Build the To-Do List.

microProjects.title: 3. Expense Tracker (UI + Local Data)
microProjects.whatToBuild: An app that accepts an expense name and amount, stores it in LocalStorage, and updates the total budget.
microProjects.action: After learning Arrays/Objects -> Build Expense Tracker. Ensure data persists on page refresh.

microProjects.title: 4. CLI Calculator
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write a Node.js script using the built-in readline module that takes two numbers and an operator from the terminal and returns the result.

microProjects.title: 5. File Promise Wrapper
microProjects.whatToBuild: Create a script that reads a text file using callbacks (fs.readFile), wraps that callback in a custom Promise, and consumes it using async/await to print contents.

advancedProject.title: Client-Side Kanban Board
advancedProject.features: Drag and drop tasks between "To Do", "In Progress", and "Done" columns. Persist data in LocalStorage.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: HTML5 Drag and Drop API, complex object state management, dynamic DOM rendering.

milestoneProject.title: Milestone Project 1: The CLI Task Tracker
milestoneProject.goal: Build a Command Line Interface (CLI) application in Node.js that lets a user add, list, update, and delete tasks.
milestoneProject.requirements: Constraint: You cannot use a database yet. You must store the tasks in a local tasks.json file using the fs/promises module. Every time you run a command, it must read the JSON, update the array, and write it back to the file.

openSource.whatToChange: Find broken links in popular list repositories and submit a PR with the corrected link.
openSource.whereToContribute: MunGell/awesome-for-beginners (look for the HTML/CSS or Markdown repos).

resources.read: Part 1 (Fundamentals, Objects, Data Types, Advanced Work with Functions). Chapters 2 through 6. Focus heavily on Objects, Closures, and Scope.
resources.skip: Generators, Modules, Promisification, Document, Events, Interfaces.

resources.read: Core interfaces in the DOM.
Read entirely.
Watch 'What the heck is the event loop anyway?'. Watch one, then close YouTube.
Introduction to Node.js, How Node.js exits, Difference between Node.js and the Browser.
Read the documentation for fs/promises.

weeks.weekTitle: WEEK 3: Async JS, HTTP, and Express.js
weeks.focus: Handling asynchronous operations, understanding HTTP protocols, and building your first backend servers.
concepts: Fetch API & HTTP Methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
HTTP Messages, Headers, Bodies, Status Codes

microProjects.title: 1. Public API Explorer
microProjects.whatToBuild: A CLI tool that fetches data from the GitHub API and logs a user's repository count and bio.
microProjects.conceptsUsed: fetch, async/await, JSON parsing, Error handling (try/catch).
microProjects.action: After learning Async JS -> Build the API Explorer. If the user doesn't exist, handle the 404 error gracefully.

microProjects.title: 2. Simple Notes API
microProjects.whatToBuild: A REST API to create, read, update, and delete text notes (stored in memory/array).
microProjects.conceptsUsed: express(), app.get(), app.post(), req.body, HTTP Status Codes.
microProjects.action: After learning Express -> Build the Notes API. Test it using Postman or cURL.

microProjects.title: 3. In-Memory REST API
microProjects.whatToBuild: Build an Express server with 4 routes (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE) that manages an array of "Users" in memory. Use Postman to test routes.

microProjects.title: 4. The Microservice Handshake (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: Modify Node.js/Express server to have route /api/ai-data. Use fetch() to call Python FastAPI server running on port 8000, retrieve JSON, and send it back to the user.
microProjects.action: Bridge Track A and Track B.

advancedProject.title: Cross-Language Integration System
advancedProject.features: Frontend HTML form -> Node.js backend -> Internal API call to FastAPI -> Returns sentiment score -> Node.js back to frontend.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Microservices communication, CORS, Server-to-Server HTTP requests via Node fetch.

openSource.whatToChange: Add a missing endpoint variation in a beginner Node.js repository. Also, find 2 repos using Express.js and read their code and folder structure.
openSource.contributionFlow: Fork -> Branch -> Write Code -> Test -> PR.
openSource.whereToContribute: GitHub issues labeled good first issue AND node, Good First Issue site.

resources.read: How async JS works, Promises, async/await.
Overview, HTTP Messages, HTTP request methods (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE).
Hello world and Basic routing.
Read this twice. Middleware is the heart of backend architecture.

weeks.focus: Storing persistent data and managing database connections in Node.js.
concepts: PostgreSQL (Relational) & Basic SQL Queries (SELECT, INSERT, JOIN)

microProjects.title: 1. MongoDB CRUD API
microProjects.whatToBuild: Upgrade Week 3's Notes API to save notes permanently in MongoDB.
microProjects.action: After learning MongoDB -> Build the CRUD API.

microProjects.title: 2. SQL Schema Architect
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write raw SQL scripts to create a users table and a posts table, then fetch a user and all their posts.
microProjects.conceptsUsed: CREATE TABLE, FOREIGN KEY, INNER JOIN.
microProjects.action: After learning SQL basics -> Build the Schema Architect.

microProjects.title: 3. The CRUD Logger
microProjects.whatToBuild: Build an Express server connected to MongoDB via Mongoose. Create a Log model (String message, Date timestamp). Create routes to POST a new log and GET all logs.

microProjects.title: 4. The AI Audit Trail (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: Update the microservice handshake. When user hits Node.js /api/ai-data, call FastAPI. Node takes prediction, saves it to MongoDB Atlas as an 'AuditRecord', and returns success.

advancedProject.title: AI Feedback Logger System
advancedProject.features: Node server queries FastAPI for classification, Node saves both text and classification to MongoDB.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: API composition, Database persistence, Error handling.

milestoneProject.title: Milestone Project 2: The Persistent Todo API
milestoneProject.goal: A complete, production-ready backend API for a Todo application.
milestoneProject.requirements: Express.js, MongoDB Atlas with Mongoose. User schema and Todo schema. Full CRUD routes. Standardized JSON error handling. No frontend yet. Document how to use in README.md.

openSource.whatToChange: Add unit tests to untested utility functions. Clone a well-maintained open-source Node/Express project, run npm install, and successfully start their server locally.
openSource.contributionFlow: Fork -> Branch -> Write tests -> Commit -> PR.

resources.read: Insert, Query, Update, and Delete Documents.
Schemas, Models, Queries.

weeks.focus: Enforcing strict data types in your backend, replacing raw SQL/Mongoose with Prisma ORM.
concepts: TypeScript Fundamentals (Static typing, Interfaces, Generics, Enums)
Prisma ORM (Schema definition, Migrations, Prisma Client)

microProjects.title: 1. Type-Safe Notes API
microProjects.whatToBuild: Rewrite your Express.js backend entirely in TypeScript.
microProjects.action: After learning TS Basics -> Build the Type-Safe Notes API.

microProjects.title: 2. Prisma Blog Schema
microProjects.whatToBuild: Define a relational schema with Users and Posts. Run migrations.
microProjects.action: After learning Prisma -> Build the Database Seeder.

microProjects.title: 3. The Typed SQL API
microProjects.whatToBuild: Rebuild Express server. Use tsc to compile. Connect to Neon/Supabase Postgres via Prisma. Create User/Post relations. Build GET/POST routes.

microProjects.title: 4. The Type-Safe Analyzer (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: Create a TS Express route that sends text to FastAPI, enforces the AIResponse type on the result, and saves text and sentiment score into Postgres via Prisma.

advancedProject.title: Type-Safe AI Router
advancedProject.features: TS backend receives payload, validates using TS interfaces, saves to PostgreSQL, forwards to FastAPI.

openSource.whatToChange: Add missing TS interfaces to a JS repository. Look at Prisma's schema.prisma examples in their test folders to see how world-class engineers write types.

resources.read: The Basics, Everyday Types, Narrowing.
Quickstart, Schema Modeling, Prisma Client queries.
Section 1 (Querying Data) and Section 4 (Managing Tables). Understand SELECT, JOIN, CREATE TABLE, foreign keys.

weeks.focus: Securing routes, organizing complex codebases into workspaces.

microProjects.title: 1. Secure Auth Flow
microProjects.whatToBuild: A Node/TS server with /signup (hashes password) and /login (verifies hash, returns JWT).
microProjects.action: After learning JWT -> Build the Auth Flow. Protect a /me route.

microProjects.title: 2. Turborepo Scaffold
microProjects.whatToBuild: Set up a Turborepo containing apps/api and packages/db.
microProjects.action: After learning Turborepo -> Scaffold your workspace.

microProjects.title: 3. The Bun/Turbo Monorepo
microProjects.whatToBuild: Initialize new Turborepo using Bun as package manager. Create apps/api and packages/types. Import a type from packages/types into apps/api.

advancedProject.title: Authenticated AI Image Service
advancedProject.techStack: Turborepo, Node.js (TS), Prisma, JWT, FastAPI, PyTorch.
advancedProject.features: User logs in, sends image. Node validates JWT, sends to FastAPI. FastAPI processes image and returns digit.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Auth Middleware, File Handling, Model Inference.

openSource.whatToChange: Implement missing JWT validation check.

resources.read: Introduction (What is JSON Web Token, How does it work).
Monorepos, Workspaces, Caching. Understand turbo.json pipeline.
What is Bun?, bun install, bun run.

weeks.focus: Shifting to declarative UI building with React.js and styling with Tailwind.

microProjects.title: 1. Component Sandbox
microProjects.whatToBuild: A static React page composed of reusable <Card /> components.

microProjects.title: 2. Dynamic Data Table
microProjects.whatToBuild: Table of user data with a real-time search bar.

microProjects.title: 3. The React Dashboard
microProjects.whatToBuild: Inside Turborepo, create apps/web using Vite+React+TS+Tailwind. Build a dashboard with sidebar and main content area. Fetch data from existing Node Todo API and render tasks.

advancedProject.title: React-Node-AI Pipeline
advancedProject.features: React frontend sends text to Node API. Node logs request, forwards to FastAPI. FastAPI returns attention score, React renders bar chart.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Full-stack wiring, CORS, React controlled components.

openSource.whatToChange: Fix accessibility issues in a React component library. Study shadcn/ui to see how world-class frontend engineers combine React properties, Tailwind utilities, and TS interfaces.

resources.read: Describing the UI, Adding Interactivity, Managing State. Pay extreme attention to useState and useEffect.
Utility-First Fundamentals, Handling Hover, Focus, Responsive Design.

weeks.focus: Connecting React to your backend APIs asynchronously.

microProjects.title: 1. Async Feed Reader
microProjects.whatToBuild: React component that fetches a list of posts from Node API on initial load.
microProjects.action: Build the Feed Reader. Trigger deliberate error to practice handling state.

microProjects.title: 2. Multi-Page SPA
microProjects.whatToBuild: Add navigation to Vite app with three pages: Home, Dashboard, Login.

microProjects.title: 3. The Agentic Chat UI (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: Build a simple chat interface in React app. Connect through Node.js to Python Agent. Manage loading states in React.

advancedProject.title: Production-Grade Sentiment Analyzer
advancedProject.techStack: React, React Router, Node.js (TS), FastAPI, HuggingFace.
advancedProject.features: React UI fetches text. Node validates, FastAPI uses HuggingFace to get sentiment, saves to PostgreSQL, returns to React.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Pre-trained AI integration, complex state management.

openSource.whatToChange: Solve infinite re-render due to missing dependency array.

resources.read: Synchronizing with Effects, You Might Not Need an Effect.

weeks.focus: Moving from vanilla React to Next.js for SSR.

microProjects.title: 1. Next.js App Scaffold
microProjects.whatToBuild: Next.js app with nested routes and persistent layout.

microProjects.title: 2. Bun API Migration
microProjects.whatToBuild: Run existing TS Express API using Bun instead of Node.

microProjects.title: 3. The Server-Rendered Dashboard
microProjects.whatToBuild: Initialize Next.js app in Turborepo. Rebuild React Todo dashboard. Fetch initial list of Todos directly in a Server Component using Prisma. Use Client Components only for buttons.

advancedProject.title: Semantic Search Engine V1
advancedProject.features: Next.js queries Bun. Bun forwards to FastAPI to calculate embeddings and return top matches.

openSource.whatToChange: Fix outdated React Router patterns in Next.js boilerplates.

resources.read: Routing, Pages, Layouts.
Read Server Components and Client Components. Must know when to use 'use client'.
Learn how fetch is extended in Next.js to handle caching.

weeks.focus: Building server actions and integrating RAG architecture into the Next.js framework.

microProjects.title: 1. Next.js File Uploader
microProjects.whatToBuild: Next.js form uploading a txt file to Bun backend.

microProjects.title: 2. Context Retriever Endpoint (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: Update FastAPI to return raw text chunks. Call this endpoint from your Turborepo Node.js backend.

advancedProject.title: Full-Stack Document Q&A App
advancedProject.features: User uploads doc. Bun saves metadata, FastAPI chunks and stores in ChromaDB. User queries text from vector store.

milestoneProject.title: Milestone Project 3: The Codeforces Clone (Skeleton Phase)
milestoneProject.goal: Begin building a competitive programming platform architecture.
milestoneProject.requirements: Monorepo, Next.js App Router, Express.js microservice. Postgres DB with Prisma tables for User, Problem, Submission. Build UI to list problems. Do not build code execution yet.

openSource.whatToChange: Add 'How to use with Bun' example to docs.

resources.read: Forms and Mutations.

weeks.focus: Bidirectional real-time communication.

microProjects.title: 1. Echo Terminal
microProjects.whatToBuild: Raw HTML client sending messages to Bun server via WS, broadcasting back.

microProjects.title: 2. Live Cursor Tracker
microProjects.whatToBuild: Next.js page showing multiple user cursors.

microProjects.title: 3. The Multi-Player Cursor Arena
microProjects.whatToBuild: Build Next.js page and Node.js/Socket.io server capturing (x,y) coordinates and emitting to server 10 times a second. Server broadcasts to all users.

advancedProject.title: Real-Time Agentic Chatbot
advancedProject.features: WebSocket stream between Next.js and Bun. FastAPI generates response and streams tokens back.

openSource.whatToChange: Add connection retry logic to chat app. Study Excalidraw repo handling real-time sync.

resources.read: Initialization, Emitting Events. Entire tutorial.
Concepts and usage, Signaling, ICE Candidates.

weeks.focus: Handling long-running tasks asynchronously.

microProjects.title: 1. The Task Spawner
microProjects.whatToBuild: Bun API pushing jobs to Redis queue. Worker processes it.

microProjects.title: 2. The Background Emailer
microProjects.whatToBuild: Build API route accepting user registration. Push user data to BullMQ backed by Upstash Redis instead of blocking. Create worker script to consume queue and log success.

advancedProject.title: Asynchronous RAG Video Processing System
advancedProject.features: Upload large transcript. Bun queues it via BullMQ. FastAPI embeds chunks. Notify UI via WS when done.

openSource.whatToChange: Improve BullMQ documentation. Look at Cal.com repo packages/trpc to see webhooks and queues.

resources.read: Installation, What an in-memory data store is, message broker basics.
Queues, Workers, Events, Getting Started, Concepts.

weeks.focus: Isolating apps into containers.

microProjects.title: 1. The Backend Container
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write Dockerfile for Bun API, build and run on mapped port.

microProjects.title: 2. The 4-Service Symphony
microProjects.whatToBuild: Spin up Postgres, Redis, Bun, and FastAPI in one compose file.

microProjects.title: 3. The Containerized Stack
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write Dockerfiles for Turborepo Node.js and FastAPI. Write docker-compose.yml at root that spins up Node, Python, Postgres, and Redis simultaneously.

advancedProject.title: Containerized Custom AI Microservice
advancedProject.features: Unified docker-compose boots entire stack. FastAPI loads LoRA model. Bun queries it.

openSource.whatToChange: Add Dockerfile to repo missing one.

resources.read: Part 1 (Orientation), Part 2 (Containers), Part 7 (Multi-container apps).
Read carefully for writing Node and Python Dockerfiles.

weeks.focus: Automating testing workflows.

microProjects.title: 1. The Strict CI Gatekeeper
microProjects.whatToBuild: Create main.yml. On push, install deps, type-check, run unit tests.

advancedProject.title: Automated AI Quality Pipeline
advancedProject.features: Trigger action on PR. Run PyTest suite that queries RAG endpoint. Evaluate responses.

openSource.whatToChange: Update deprecated GitHub Actions.

resources.read: Writing your first workflow, Actions basics.

weeks.focus: Pushing containers to public internet.

microProjects.title: 1. The Split Deployment
microProjects.whatToBuild: Deploy Next.js to Vercel, Bun to Railway. Setup CORS.

microProjects.title: 2. Nginx Gatekeeper
microProjects.whatToBuild: Spin up Linux VPS. Install Docker/Nginx. Route port 80 to internal Docker ports.

microProjects.title: 3. The Edge Frontend
microProjects.whatToBuild: Buy dev domain. Connect Turborepo to Vercel. Deploy Next.js. Configure Vercel ENV to point to production Postgres instead of local.

microProjects.title: 4. The Production Matrix (The Glue)
microProjects.whatToBuild: SSH into DigitalOcean. Install Git, Docker, Nginx. Clone Turborepo. Run docker-compose in detached mode. Configure Nginx and use Certbot for free SSL HTTPS.

advancedProject.title: Production-Grade RAG API
advancedProject.features: Public Node API routes to internal FastAPI running Re-Ranking.

openSource.whatToChange: Add 'Deploy to Railway' or Docker+Nginx guide.

resources.read: Getting Started, Deployments, Environment Variables, Root Directory configs.
Provisioning Droplets, SSH, UFW Firewall (Ports 22, 80, 443).
Proxying traffic, nginx.conf structure.

weeks.focus: Optimizing UX with SSE.

microProjects.title: 1. SSE Token Streamer
microProjects.whatToBuild: Bun endpoint streaming words via text/event-stream.

advancedProject.title: Streaming Multi-Agent Research Dashboard
advancedProject.features: Next.js SSE to Bun. FastAPI runs multi-agent workflow, streams status back to UI.

openSource.whatToChange: Implement stop generation button.

resources.read: Receiving events.

weeks.focus: Replacing REST with Protocol Buffers.

microProjects.title: 1. The Polyglot Proto
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write service.proto. Generate TS and Python classes via protoc.

microProjects.title: 2. Redis Cache Middleware
microProjects.whatToBuild: Add Redis middleware to slow Express route. Return instantly if cached.

advancedProject.title: Ultra-Fast Multimodal Inspector
advancedProject.features: Bun sends image/audio via gRPC to FastAPI. Processed via Whisper/VLM, returned via gRPC.

openSource.whatToChange: Implement Protobufs for slow REST APIs.

resources.read: Core Concepts.

weeks.focus: Ensuring database survives high traffic.
concepts: Database Indexing (B-Trees) and Query Plans (EXPLAIN ANALYZE)

microProjects.title: 1. The Million Row Indexer
microProjects.whatToBuild: Insert 1M rows. Use EXPLAIN ANALYZE. Add Index and compare times.

microProjects.title: 2. Nginx Load Balancer
microProjects.whatToBuild: Spin up 3 Bun instances. Route via Nginx Round-Robin.

advancedProject.title: GraphRAG Deep Research Engine
advancedProject.features: Extract Knowledge Graph. Query Neo4j + ChromaDB together.

openSource.whatToChange: Add missing DB indexes to Prisma schema.

resources.read: Anatomy of an Index, B-Tree implementation.
Proxying Traffic, Choosing a Load-Balancing Method.

weeks.focus: Visualizing health of microservices.

microProjects.title: 1. The Metric Exporter
microProjects.whatToBuild: Expose /metrics tracking HTTP requests and latency.

microProjects.title: 2. The Command Center
microProjects.whatToBuild: Docker-compose Prometheus/Grafana. Visualize RPS.

advancedProject.title: Monitored AI Gateway
advancedProject.features: Node API gateway tracks latency. Visualized in Grafana.

openSource.whatToChange: Add OpenTelemetry tracing to a project.

resources.read: Concepts, Overview, Getting Started.
Sentry crash course for tracking exact line of code bugs.

weeks.focus: Locking down full-stack API against malicious actors.

microProjects.title: 1. The Gated Gateway
microProjects.whatToBuild: Implement GitHub OAuth in Next.js via Auth.js. Add Upstash Rate Limiting to WebSocket endpoint (5 msgs/min). Return 429.

advancedProject.title: Real-Time Voice-to-Voice AI Agent
advancedProject.features: Voice streams via WebRTC, converted to text, responded via TTS, streamed back.

milestoneProject.title: Milestone Project 4: The Devin Clone (Skeleton Phase)
milestoneProject.goal: Begin building an autonomous software engineering agent.
milestoneProject.requirements: Split-screen React UI (Chat/Terminal). MCP Server attached to Python backend accessing an isolated Docker container. Agent must write script, execute via tool, read output.

openSource.whatToChange: Study NextAuth.js repo. Check how they enforce cookies and prevent XSS.

resources.read: Broken Access Control, Cryptographic Failures, Injection.
Setup guide for Next.js App Router.
Token Bucket algorithm via Redis.

weeks.focus: Orchestrating containers via K8s.

microProjects.title: 1. The Local Cluster
microProjects.whatToBuild: Install Minikube. Write deployment for 3 Bun replicas.

microProjects.title: 2. Internal Service Discovery
microProjects.whatToBuild: Bun communicates with FastAPI via internal K8s DNS.

advancedProject.title: K8s-Deployed Agentic Swarm
advancedProject.features: LoadBalancer Service routes Bun replicas. Internal DNS to FastAPI.

openSource.whatToChange: Add deployment/service yamls to projects.

resources.read: API, Components.

weeks.focus: Serverless Edge vs Origin.

microProjects.title: 1. Edge Auth Middleware
microProjects.whatToBuild: Next.js Edge Middleware intercepts request and verifies JWT globally.

microProjects.title: 2. Global Latency Router
microProjects.whatToBuild: Route EU users to eu-central API, US to us-east.

advancedProject.title: Edge-Protected Enterprise AI Gateway
advancedProject.features: Edge checks JWT, Bun rate limits, FastAPI filters AI output.

openSource.whatToChange: Add NeMo guardrails example.

resources.read: Middleware overview.

weeks.focus: Large scale architecture.

microProjects.title: 1. Token Bucket Rate Limiter
microProjects.whatToBuild: Redis rate limit. 5 req/min. Return 429.

microProjects.title: 2. System Architecture Whiteboard
microProjects.whatToBuild: Draw diagram for 1M concurrent users.

advancedProject.title: Production Load Tester
advancedProject.features: Simulate 1,000 users. Watch it crash, fix bottlenecks.

openSource.whatToChange: Fix N+1 query problem.

resources.read: CAP, Proxies.

weeks.focus: Resume-defining Capstone.

advancedProject.title: The Final Capstone
advancedProject.features: Choose one: AI Codeforces Clone, Autonomous Research SaaS, Real-Time Audio Translator.

milestoneProject.title: Milestone Project 4: Deploy the Devin Clone
milestoneProject.goal: Autonomous AI engineer fully live on internet and observable.
milestoneProject.requirements: Vercel frontend. DigitalOcean backend API and Python. Python spins up node:alpine isolated Docker containers. Sentry integrated for full stack trace. Custom RunPod endpoint for AI reasoning.

openSource.whatToChange: Open source your Capstone project. Find 3 early-stage foreign startups on YC Work at a Startup, reproduce a bug using Docker, submit PR.

resources.read: The entire guide.
Resume formatting, Action bullet points.

weeks.focus: Shifting from 100% learning to a 50/50 split of continued building and aggressive, targeted networking.

microProjects.title: 1. The Proof of Work Portfolio
microProjects.whatToBuild: A single-page Next.js portfolio showcasing exactly THREE projects: Capstone, Multi-Agent System, and High-Performance Microservices app.
microProjects.action: Write 3-bullet summary for each: tech stack, hardest technical problem solved.

microProjects.title: 2. The Asynchronous Cold Email Engine
microProjects.whatToBuild: Spreadsheet tracking 50 foreign startups. Find CTO/VP of Engineering emails.
microProjects.action: Send 4-sentence email: Who you are, Capstone link, specific bug/feature in their product, low-friction CTA.

microProjects.title: 3. System Design Verbalization
microProjects.whatToBuild: Record a 5-minute Loom video walking through Excalidraw architecture of Capstone.
microProjects.action: Explain architectural choices (Bun vs Node, ChromaDB). Send link in cold emails.

advancedProject.title: The Paid Trial / Contract-to-Hire
advancedProject.features: Remove hiring risk for foreign companies.
advancedProject.conceptsUsed: Pitch a 4-week paid trial contract to take a scoped ticket from their backlog.

openSource.whatToContribute: Core features to a startup's public repo.
openSource.whatToChange: Find an early-stage AI startup. Solve 2-3 complex issues, write tests, submit PRs.
openSource.contributionFlow: Once merged, directly DM the founder on Twitter/LinkedIn to ask for a role.

resources.read: Filter by 'Remote' and 'Engineering'. Look for Seed/Series A.
Create profile focusing entirely on startup roles.
Templates for passing engineering manager screens.

weeks.focus: Mastering engineering communication, MLOps, and reading academic AI literature to stay ahead of the curve.

microProjects.title: 1. The Architecture RFC
microProjects.whatToBuild: Write a 3-page Markdown document for a new Capstone feature detailing Architecture, DB Schema, API Contracts, Alternative Approaches.
microProjects.action: Send it to another developer/LLM for critique before coding.

microProjects.title: 2. The Blameless Post-Mortem
microProjects.whatToBuild: Deliberately crash Capstone DB. Write RCA document using '5 Whys' technique.

advancedProject.title: The Internal Workflow Automator
advancedProject.features: Find the most annoying task your new team does. Build an internal tool connecting company systems to LLM via FastAPI.

openSource.whatToChange: Stop just submitting PRs. Review other people's PRs, test code, find edge cases, leave feedback.
openSource.contributionFlow: Find open PR -> Pull branch locally -> Run tests -> Leave detailed review.
openSource.whereToContribute: Repos where you have successful merges.

resources.read: The entire article. Exactly how Google engineers write design docs.
Blameless Post-Mortems, Best Practices.

weeks.focus: Moving beyond single-threaded limitations by learning Go and Rust for WASM to achieve maximum throughput.

microProjects.title: 1. The High-Throughput Go Router
microProjects.whatToBuild: Go web server endpoint accepting 1,000 IDs. Spawn 1,000 Goroutines simulating DB reads. Collect via Channel.
microProjects.action: Build Go Router and compare memory footprint.

microProjects.title: 2. Rust-to-WASM Image Filter
microProjects.whatToBuild: Rust function processing raw image byte array with grayscale conversion. Compile to WASM. Load in Next.js.

advancedProject.title: The Hybrid Inference Gateway
advancedProject.features: Next.js uses Rust WASM to compress payload. Go API Gateway uses Goroutines to route request to 5 FastAPI workers, waits for fastest response.

openSource.whatToChange: Find Python/Node CLI tool. Rewrite core bottleneck in Go/Rust and submit PR.

resources.read: Basics, Methods and interfaces, Concurrency.
Introduction, Hello World, Setup.

weeks.focus: Dropping below PyTorch to write custom GPU instructions, training models across multiple machines.

microProjects.title: 1. The Go gRPC Streamer
microProjects.whatToBuild: Upgrade gRPC proto with 'stream' keyword. Python client streams 100 audio chunks/sec to Go server, Go streams text back.

advancedProject.title: Distributed PEFT Trainer
advancedProject.features: Multi-process training loop fine-tuning LLM via LoRA. Replaces PyTorch activation with custom Triton kernel.

openSource.whatToChange: Implement Triton kernel fallback in AI repository, or fix device placement bug.
openSource.contributionFlow: Fork -> Branch -> Debug multi-GPU logic -> PR.

resources.read: What is DDP, Single Node Multi-GPU setup.

weeks.focus: Leveraging academic research for industry credibility, ruthlessly optimizing hardware limits, positioning as Senior talent.
concepts: The Research Leverage (Bridging academic research and production software)
Professional Signaling (Updating LinkedIn/GitHub emphasizing TS, Node, Architecture)

microProjects.title: 1. The Production SAR Pipeline
microProjects.whatToBuild: Wrap PyTorch oil spill detection model in production-grade microservice. FastAPI inference, TS/Node backend.
microProjects.action: Push heavy compute to cloud pipelines.

microProjects.title: 2. The Rock-Solid Environment
microProjects.whatToBuild: Frozen, unbreakable Debian 13 XFCE setup. Stick to X11.

microProjects.title: 3. The Deep Tech Cold Pitch
microProjects.whatToBuild: Find 20 foreign startups in Earth Observation/Computer Vision. Cold email CTOs.

advancedProject.title: The Marathon Mindset
advancedProject.features: Maintain 10km distance running. Sync training blocks: 4 hrs code, run, 4 hrs code.
